title: Substance Use Disorder Referrals - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Substance Use Disorder Referrals 35 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Medicaid Recipients Veterans ADVANCED HEALTH COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Advanced Health is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Advanced Health Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 269-7400 Visit Website More Details ADVANCED HEALTH FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 269-7400 Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH BROOKINGS OFFICE COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 471-4106 Visit Website More Details ALLCARE HEALTH ALLCARE HEALTH MEDFORD OFFICE COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF AllCare CCO coordinates health insurance services for Medicaid members assigned to its Coordinated Care Organization (CCO). Includes specialized information and referrals to healthcare providers for medical, dental, and behavioral health. Additional wellness benefits for AllCare CCO members include medical transportation, pain management, weight management programs, and a 24/7 nurse help line. AllCare CCO members can receive vouchers from providers for taking healthy actions such as attending appointments and tobacco cessation, to purchase items at the BABE Store such as diapers, books, and blankets. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (888) 460-0185 Visit Website More Details BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK OF LANE COUNTY RAPID RESOURCE & TREATMENT ACCESS HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a single point of access to substance use treatment, harm reduction, and recovery resources. Services are offered by a collaborative group of local providers. Partner agencies may also be able to help with additional services such as housing support, job finding assistance, peer support, mental health support, needle exchange, and STI testing. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 422-2595 Visit Website More Details CAREOREGON COORDINATED HEALTH CARE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF CareOregon is a Managed Care Organization (MCO) that coordinates Medicaid services for Coordinated Care Organizations (CCO) Jackson Care Connect and Columbia Pacific. CareOregon also provides health insurance services through its partnering CCO, Health Share. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, substance use disorder treatment, and non-emergency transportation for health care visits covered by the CCO. Also provides job search assistance such as preparing a resume, completing job applications, and interview techniques. Other services include: counseling including anger management, detox and rehab referrals, Oregon Health Plan enrollment assistance, and computer and internet access. 1100 Jackson Street SE Albany, OR Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Columbia Pacific CCO Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 488-2822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA PACIFIC CCO FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 416-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE DEFLECTION DRUG TREATMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps people quickly access drug treatment. People can ask for help even if they are not in legal trouble. Police officers can sometimes take individuals directly to treatment instead of jail. The program works with the court, Parole and Probation, the District Attorney’s Office, and defense lawyers to support people who need treatment. Participants are paired with a peer navigator and receive case management/wraparound services. Services may include help with obtaining birth certificates and IDs, building resumes, finding jobs, housing and transportation support, signing up for health insurance, medication-assisted treatment, detox services, coordination with ODHS, and referrals to other resources. 63333 W Highway 20 Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6:30pm Email (541) 585-0008 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Eastern Oregon Coordinated Care Organization (EOCCO) is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. Provides specialized information and referrals to health care providers, including physicians and specialists, for services such as dental care, eye care, pharmacies, substance use disorder treatment, and transportation for health care visits covered by the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Eastern Oregon Coordinated Care Organization Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (503) 765-3521 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (for individuals being discharged from the hospital or who have a set move-in date for a new home and need a temporary place to stay before then) -Electronics such as cell phones, computers, and other communication devices when there are no state programs available in the area -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships or home equipment -Beds and mattresses for children under 21 or for adults with a diagnosis of a back or spine condition -Car seats if there are no other county or programs available in the area All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Visit website to review additional eligibility criteria regarding specific programs and benefits. Eligibility: Medicaid/Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members enrolled in Health Share of Oregon; members are able to choose medical care through CareOregon, Kaiser Permanente, Legacy Health, OHSU, or Providence. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:45pm Email (503) 416-8090 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INFORMATIONAL RECORD COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) is a local Oregon Health Plan (OHP) or (Medicaid) plan that helps OHP members use their benefits. CCOs are made up of all types of healthcare providers in a community. They work together to care for OHP members in an area or region of the state. If your caller has OHP, remember to suggest contacting their CCO to find out which providers or services are covered under their plan. Eligibility: Oregon Health Plan recipients Hours: Not applicable Visit Website More Details INTERCOMMUNITY HEALTH NETWORK CCO COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF InterCommunity Health Network is a Coordinated Care Organization (CCO) that coordinates health care services for Medicaid members assigned to its CCO. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. 500 Summer Street NE E-49 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. VA providers know that, in many cases, substance use problems are continuing conditions that require care over a long period of time. Services may include short-term case management, onsite primary care, access to medical, mental health, and substance abuse treatment, and access to VA housing programs, including Grant and Per Diem program, HUD/VASH, Veterans Recovery House and Supportive Service for Veteran Families (SSVF) if found eligible. These grants do not provide immediate assistance, are sometimes one-time, and may include case management. Help with finding market rate or subsidized housing or linkage to community housing programs. Assistance with expediting VA Benefit claims and access to employment programs such as Supported Employment, Veterans Reintegration program, and State employment. Access to computers, clothing, and hygiene items is available onsite. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Veterans must be present in the household and must be the one to request services. Some services available for Veterans who are not eligible for full VA Healthcare benefits. Hours: -Business hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 7:30am-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays -Walk-in hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-3pm, Thursday 10am-3pm. No walk-ins after 2:30pm, better to go earlier in the day. Closed federal holidays.
